web_text: Paul, an apostle (not from men, nor through man, but through Jesus Christ, and God the Father, who raised him from the dead), and all the brothers who are with me, to the assemblies of Galatia: Grace to you and peace from God the Father, and our Lord Jesus Christ, who gave himself for our sins, that he might deliver us out of this present evil age, according to the will of our God and Father—
slang_text: It's Paul here, an apostle — and not 'cause some dude appointed me, not through any human channel, but straight through Jesus Christ and God the Father, who raised Him up from the dead — plus all the brothers hangin' with me, writing to the churches out in Galatia. Grace and peace to you, from God the Father and our Lord Jesus Christ, who handed Himself over for our sins so He could bust us out of this bogus, evil age we're stuck in, all accordin' to the will of our God and Father —

web_text: to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen. I marvel that you are so quickly deserting him who called you in the grace of Christ to a different “good news”; and there isn’t another “good news.” Only there are some who trouble you, and want to pervert the Good News of Christ. But even though we, or an angel from heaven, should preach to you any “good news” other than that which we preached to you, let him be cursed.
slang_text: to Him be the glory forever and ever. Amen. I'm totally blown away that you're bailin' on Him this fast, the one who called you into Christ's grace, chasin' after some different "good news" — and there ain't no other good news out there, no way. It's just some clowns stirrin' up trouble, tryin' to twist up the real Good News about Christ. But even if we ourselves, or an angel straight outta heaven, came preachin' any good news different from what we already preached to you, let 'em be cursed. That's how bogus it'd be.

web_text: As we have said before, so I now say again: if any man preaches to you any “good news” other than that which you received, let him be cursed. For am I now seeking the favor of men, or of God? Or am I striving to please men? For if I were still pleasing men, I wouldn’t be a servant of Christ. But I make known to you, brothers, concerning the Good News which was preached by me, that it is not according to man. For neither did I receive it from man, nor was I taught it, but it came to me through revelation of Jesus Christ.
slang_text: Like we already said, I'm sayin' it again: if any dude preaches you a different good news than the one you already got, let him be cursed. Am I out here chasin' brownie points from people, or from God? You think I'm tryin' to please folks? 'Cause if I was still tryin' to please people, I wouldn't be no servant of Christ. But I'm layin' it out plain for you, brothers: the Good News I preached ain't some man-made deal. I didn't get it from a human, nobody taught it to me — it came straight to me through a revelation of Jesus Christ Himself.

web_text: For you have heard of my way of living in time past in the Jews’ religion, how that beyond measure I persecuted the assembly of God, and ravaged it. I advanced in the Jews’ religion beyond many of my own age among my countrymen, being more exceedingly zealous for the traditions of my fathers. But when it was the good pleasure of God, who separated me from my mother’s womb, and called me through his grace, to reveal his Son in me, that I might preach him among the Gentiles, I didn’t immediately confer with flesh and blood,
slang_text: You've already heard about how I used to live it up back in the Jewish religion, how I went after God's church way past reasonable, straight-up tryin' to wreck it. I was outpacin' tons of guys my own age among my own people, way more fired up over my ancestors' old traditions than any of 'em. But when God — who set me apart before I was even born, and called me through His grace — decided the time was right to reveal His Son in me, so I could preach Him to the Gentiles, I didn't go runnin' to check in with anybody, no flesh-and-blood consultin' whatsoever,

web_text: nor did I go up to Jerusalem to those who were apostles before me, but I went away into Arabia. Then I returned to Damascus. Then after three years I went up to Jerusalem to visit Peter, and stayed with him fifteen days. But of the other apostles I saw no one, except James, the Lord’s brother. Now about the things which I write to you, behold, before God, I’m not lying.
slang_text: and I didn't even head up to Jerusalem to check with the apostles who came before me — I bounced off to Arabia instead. Then I circled back to Damascus. Three years later I finally went up to Jerusalem to hang with Peter, stayin' with him fifteen days. Didn't lay eyes on any other apostle 'cept James, the Lord's own brother. Now about everything I'm writin' you here — check it, before God, I'm not lyin' one bit.

web_text: Then I came to the regions of Syria and Cilicia. I was still unknown by face to the assemblies of Judea which were in Christ, but they only heard: “He who once persecuted us now preaches the faith that he once tried to destroy.” And they glorified God in me.
slang_text: After that I headed out to Syria and Cilicia. The churches in Judea still didn't know me by face, only by reputation — they just heard, "That dude who used to hunt us down is now preachin' the very faith he tried to wreck." And they gave God the glory over me, big time.

web_text: Then after a period of fourteen years I went up again to Jerusalem with Barnabas, taking Titus also with me. I went up by revelation, and I laid before them the Good News which I preach among the Gentiles, but privately before those who were respected, for fear that I might be running, or had run, in vain. But not even Titus, who was with me, being a Greek, was compelled to be circumcised. This was because of the false brothers secretly brought in, who stole in to spy out our liberty which we have in Christ Jesus, that they might bring us into bondage;
slang_text: Then fourteen years later, I headed back up to Jerusalem with Barnabas, draggin' Titus along too. I went up 'cause of a revelation, and I laid out the Good News I preach to the Gentiles, but I did it privately with the big shots, 'cause I was scared I might be runnin', or had already run, this whole race for nothin'. But not even Titus, who was with me and a total Greek, got forced into gettin' circumcised. That whole showdown happened 'cause some fake brothers snuck in on the sly, spyin' out the freedom we've got in Christ Jesus, tryin' to drag us back into bondage;

web_text: to whom we gave no place in the way of subjection, not for an hour, that the truth of the Good News might continue with you. But from those who were reputed to be important (whatever they were, it makes no difference to me; God doesn’t show partiality to man)—they, I say, who were respected imparted nothing to me, but to the contrary, when they saw that I had been entrusted with the Good News for the uncircumcised, even as Peter with the Good News for the circumcised (for he who worked through Peter in the apostleship with the circumcised also worked through me with the Gentiles);
slang_text: and we didn't back down to those guys, not even for an hour, so the truth of the Good News would stick around for you guys. But from the ones who had big reputations (whoever they were, don't matter to me one bit — God doesn't play favorites) — those respected dudes, I'm sayin', didn't add nothin' to what I already had. If anything, it went the other way — once they saw I'd been handed the Good News for the uncircumcised, same as Peter got handed it for the circumcised (same God workin' through Peter for the Jews was workin' through me for the Gentiles);

web_text: and when they perceived the grace that was given to me, James and Cephas and John, they who were reputed to be pillars, gave to me and Barnabas the right hand of fellowship, that we should go to the Gentiles, and they to the circumcision. They only asked us to remember the poor—which very thing I was also zealous to do. But when Peter came to Antioch, I resisted him to his face, because he stood condemned. For before some people came from James, he ate with the Gentiles. But when they came, he drew back and separated himself, fearing those who were of the circumcision.
slang_text: and once they clocked the grace I'd been given, James and Cephas and John — the guys everybody figured were the pillars of the whole outfit — shook hands with me and Barnabas, both of us in, and split things up: us headin' to the Gentiles, them stickin' with the circumcised crowd. All they asked was that we remember the poor, which I was already totally fired up to do anyway. But when Peter rolled into Antioch, I called him out to his face, 'cause he was straight-up in the wrong. See, before some guys showed up from James, he was eatin' right alongside the Gentiles. But once those guys arrived, he backed off and cut himself out, scared of the circumcision crowd.

web_text: And the rest of the Jews joined him in his hypocrisy; so that even Barnabas was carried away with their hypocrisy. But when I saw that they didn’t walk uprightly according to the truth of the Good News, I said to Peter before them all, “If you, being a Jew, live as the Gentiles do, and not as the Jews do, why do you compel the Gentiles to live as the Jews do? “We, being Jews by nature, and not Gentile sinners, yet knowing that a man is not justified by the works of the law but through faith in Jesus Christ, even we believed in Christ Jesus, that we might be justified by faith in Christ, and not by the works of the law, because no flesh will be justified by the works of the law.
slang_text: And the rest of the Jewish crowd jumped on the bandwagon with his two-faced act, so even Barnabas got swept up in it. But when I saw they weren't walkin' straight with the truth of the Good News, I called Peter out in front of everybody: "If you, a Jew, are already livin' like a Gentile instead of like a Jew, what's your deal forcin' the Gentiles to live like Jews?" We're Jews by birth, not some Gentile sinners, but we already know a person doesn't get made right with God by workin' the law — it's through faith in Jesus Christ. Even we bought into Christ Jesus, so we could get made right through faith in Christ, not through workin' the law, 'cause not one single soul is gettin' made right that way.

web_text: But if, while we sought to be justified in Christ, we ourselves also were found sinners, is Christ a servant of sin? Certainly not! For if I build up again those things which I destroyed, I prove myself a law-breaker. For I, through the law, died to the law, that I might live to God. I have been crucified with Christ, and it is no longer I that live, but Christ lives in me. That life which I now live in the flesh, I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me, and gave himself up for me.
slang_text: But if, while we're tryin' to get made right in Christ, we turn up sinners ourselves too, does that make Christ some servant of sin? No way, not a chance! 'Cause if I go rebuildin' the stuff I already tore down, that just proves I'm a law-breaker. See, through the law, I died to the law, so I could live for God instead. I've been crucified right along with Christ, and it's not me livin' anymore — it's Christ livin' in me. And this life I'm livin' now in this body, I'm livin' it by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and handed Himself over for me.

web_text: I don’t reject the grace of God. For if righteousness is through the law, then Christ died for nothing!”
slang_text: I'm not tossin' out God's grace like it's nothin'. 'Cause if you could get righteous through the law, then Christ died for absolutely nothin' — and that's just bogus."

web_text: Foolish Galatians, who has bewitched you not to obey the truth, before whose eyes Jesus Christ was openly portrayed among you as crucified? I just want to learn this from you: Did you receive the Spirit by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith? Are you so foolish? Having begun in the Spirit, are you now completed in the flesh? Did you suffer so many things in vain, if it is indeed in vain?
slang_text: You dipsticks, you Galatians — who cast a spell on you to bail on the truth, when Jesus Christ got painted out plain as day right in front of your faces, crucified and all? I just wanna know one thing: did you get the Spirit by workin' the law, or by hearin' and believin'? Are you really that clueless? You started out in the Spirit, and now you're tryin' to finish up in the flesh? Did you go through all that grief for nothin', if it really is for nothin'?

web_text: He therefore who supplies the Spirit to you, and does miracles among you, does he do it by the works of the law, or by hearing of faith? Even as Abraham “believed God, and it was counted to him for righteousness.” Know therefore that those who are of faith, the same are children of Abraham. The Scripture, foreseeing that God would justify the Gentiles by faith, preached the Good News beforehand to Abraham, saying, “In you all the nations will be blessed.”
slang_text: So the guy who hands you the Spirit and works miracles among you — is He doin' that by law-keepin', or by hearin' and believin'? Same deal as Abraham, who "believed God, and it got counted to him as righteousness." So get this: whoever's runnin' on faith, those are the real children of Abraham. Scripture already saw comin' that God would make the Gentiles right through faith, so it preached the Good News to Abraham ahead of time, sayin', "Through you, every nation's gonna get blessed."

web_text: So then, those who are of faith are blessed with the faithful Abraham. For as many as are of the works of the law are under a curse. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who doesn’t continue in all things that are written in the book of the law, to do them.” Now that no man is justified by the law before God is evident, for, “The righteous will live by faith.” The law is not of faith, but, “The man who does them will live by them.”
slang_text: So bottom line, whoever's runnin' on faith gets blessed right along with faithful old Abraham. But whoever's leanin' on law-keepin' is stuck under a curse. It's written: "Cursed is everybody who doesn't keep every single thing written in the law book." It's plain as day nobody gets made right with God by the law, 'cause "the righteous will live by faith." The law's got nothin' to do with faith — it's, "whoever does these things will live by 'em."

web_text: Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law, having become a curse for us. For it is written, “Cursed is everyone who hangs on a tree,” that the blessing of Abraham might come on the Gentiles through Christ Jesus; that we might receive the promise of the Spirit through faith. Brothers, speaking of human terms, though it is only a man’s covenant, yet when it has been confirmed, no one makes it void, or adds to it. Now the promises were spoken to Abraham and to his offspring. He doesn’t say, “To descendants”, as of many, but as of one, “To your offspring”, which is Christ.
slang_text: Christ bought us back outta the law's curse by becomin' a curse Himself, for us — 'cause it's written, "Cursed is everybody who hangs on a tree." He did it so Abraham's blessing could reach the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, so we could get the promised Spirit through faith. Brothers, talkin' in plain human terms — even just a regular guy's contract, once it's locked in, nobody can void it or tack stuff onto it. Now the promises got spoken to Abraham and his offspring. God didn't say "to descendants," like a whole crowd of 'em — He said "to your offspring," singular, meanin' Christ.

web_text: Now I say this: A covenant confirmed beforehand by God in Christ, the law, which came four hundred thirty years after, does not annul, so as to make the promise of no effect. For if the inheritance is of the law, it is no more of promise; but God has granted it to Abraham by promise. Then why is there the law? It was added because of transgressions, until the offspring should come to whom the promise has been made. It was ordained through angels by the hand of a mediator. Now a mediator is not between one, but God is one.
slang_text: Here's what I'm gettin' at: a deal God already locked in with Christ can't get canceled out by the law, which showed up four hundred thirty years down the road — that wouldn't be fair, killin' the promise like that. 'Cause if the inheritance came from the law, it wouldn't be a promise no more — but God handed it to Abraham straight-up as a promise. So what's the point of the law, then? It got tacked on 'cause of sin, until the offspring the promise was made about finally showed up. It got set up through angels, by a middleman's hand. But a middleman's only needed when there's more than one side — and God's just one.

web_text: Is the law then against the promises of God? Certainly not! For if there had been a law given which could make alive, most certainly righteousness would have been of the law. But the Scriptures imprisoned all things under sin, that the promise by faith in Jesus Christ might be given to those who believe. But before faith came, we were kept in custody under the law, confined for the faith which should afterwards be revealed. So that the law has become our tutor to bring us to Christ, that we might be justified by faith.
slang_text: So is the law fightin' against God's promises? No way, not a chance! 'Cause if a law coulda been handed down that could actually give life, then righteousness woulda come straight from the law, easy. But Scripture locked everything up under sin, so the promise through faith in Jesus Christ could get handed to whoever believes. But before faith showed up, we were locked up under the law, cooped up waitin' for the faith that was gonna get revealed later. So the law turned into our babysitter, draggin' us along toward Christ, so we could get made right through faith.

web_text: But now that faith has come, we are no longer under a tutor. For you are all children of God, through faith in Christ Jesus. For as many of you as were baptized into Christ have put on Christ. There is neither Jew nor Greek, there is neither slave nor free man, there is neither male nor female; for you are all one in Christ Jesus.
slang_text: But now that faith's finally showed up, we don't need that babysitter no more. You're all God's kids now, through faith in Christ Jesus. Every last one of you who got baptized into Christ has suited up in Christ. There's no Jew or Greek in this deal, no slave or free man, no male or female — you're all one, no exceptions, in Christ Jesus.

web_text: If you are Christ’s, then you are Abraham’s offspring and heirs according to promise.
slang_text: And if you belong to Christ, then you're Abraham's own offspring, heirs to the whole promise, plain and simple.

web_text: But I say that so long as the heir is a child, he is no different from a bondservant, though he is lord of all; but is under guardians and stewards until the day appointed by the father. So we also, when we were children, were held in bondage under the elemental principles of the world. But when the fullness of the time came, God sent out his Son, born to a woman, born under the law,
slang_text: But here's the deal — as long as the heir's still a kid, he's no different than some servant, even though he's technically boss of everything. He's stuck under guardians and managers till whatever day his dad set. Same with us — back when we were kids, we were locked up under this world's basic rules. But once the time was finally ripe, God sent out His Son, born to a woman, born under the law,

web_text: that he might redeem those who were under the law, that we might receive the adoption of children. And because you are children, God sent out the Spirit of his Son into your hearts, crying, “Abba, Father!” So you are no longer a bondservant, but a son; and if a son, then an heir of God through Christ. However at that time, not knowing God, you were in bondage to those who by nature are not gods.
slang_text: so He could buy back the ones stuck under the law, so we could get adopted as full-on kids. And 'cause you're kids now, God sent out the Spirit of His Son right into your hearts, callin' out, "Abba, Father!" So you're not some servant anymore — you're a son, and if you're a son, you're an heir of God through Christ. But back when you didn't even know God yet, you were locked up servin' things that ain't even real gods.

web_text: But now that you have come to know God, or rather to be known by God, why do you turn back again to the weak and miserable elemental principles, to which you desire to be in bondage all over again? You observe days, months, seasons, and years. I am afraid for you, that I might have wasted my labor for you. I beg you, brothers, become as I am, for I also have become as you are. You did me no wrong,
slang_text: But now that you know God — or better yet, God knows you — why are you turnin' back to those weak, sorry basic rules, tryin' to get chained up all over again? You're keepin' track of days, months, seasons, years, like some superstitious calendar thing. I'm scared for you guys, scared I busted my hump for you for nothin'. I'm beggin' you, brothers, become like me, 'cause I became like you. You never wronged me none,

web_text: but you know that because of weakness in the flesh I preached the Good News to you the first time. That which was a temptation to you in my flesh, you didn’t despise nor reject; but you received me as an angel of God, even as Christ Jesus. What was the blessing you enjoyed? For I testify to you that, if possible, you would have plucked out your eyes and given them to me. So then, have I become your enemy by telling you the truth?
slang_text: but you know it was some physical weakness that got me preachin' the Good News to you the first time around. Even though my condition coulda been a total turn-off, you didn't diss me or shove me away — you welcomed me like an angel straight from God, like Christ Jesus Himself. So where'd that good feelin' go? I'll swear to it, if you coulda, you woulda gouged out your own eyes and handed 'em to me, that's how much you cared. So did I turn into your enemy just for tellin' you the truth?

web_text: They zealously seek you in no good way. No, they desire to alienate you, that you may seek them. But it is always good to be zealous in a good cause, and not only when I am present with you. My little children, of whom I am again in travail until Christ is formed in you— but I could wish to be present with you now, and to change my tone, for I am perplexed about you.
slang_text: Those guys are chasin' after you hard, but for all the wrong reasons — they wanna cut you off from us, so you'll go chasin' after them instead. It's always cool to be fired up for a good cause, not just when I happen to be around. My little dudes, I'm goin' through labor pains all over again till Christ actually takes shape in you — man, I wish I could be there with you right now and switch up my tone, 'cause honestly, you got me stumped.

web_text: Tell me, you that desire to be under the law, don’t you listen to the law? For it is written that Abraham had two sons, one by the servant, and one by the free woman. However, the son by the servant was born according to the flesh, but the son by the free woman was born through promise. These things contain an allegory, for these are two covenants. One is from Mount Sinai, bearing children to bondage, which is Hagar.
slang_text: Tell me somethin', you guys so desperate to get under the law — don't you actually listen to what the law says? It's written that Abraham had two sons, one by the slave woman, one by the free woman. The slave woman's son got born the regular fleshly way, but the free woman's son got born through a promise. There's a deeper picture in all this — these are two covenants. One's from Mount Sinai, crankin' out kids born into bondage — that's Hagar.

web_text:
For this Hagar is Mount Sinai in Arabia, and answers to the Jerusalem that exists now, for she is in bondage with her children. But the Jerusalem that is above is free, which is the mother of us all. For it is written,
“Rejoice, you barren who don’t bear.
Break out and shout, you that don’t travail.
For more are the children of the desolate than of her who has a husband.” Now we, brothers, as Isaac was, are children of promise.
slang_text: This Hagar's the same as Mount Sinai over in Arabia, matchin' up with the Jerusalem runnin' the show right now, 'cause she's stuck in bondage with all her kids. But the Jerusalem up in heaven is free — she's the mother of all of us. Like it's written: "Get stoked, you barren woman who never had kids. Bust out and shout, you who never went through labor. 'Cause the desolate woman's gonna end up with way more kids than the one who's got a husband." So we, brothers, same as Isaac, are kids born outta a promise.

web_text: But as then, he who was born according to the flesh persecuted him who was born according to the Spirit, so also it is now. However what does the Scripture say? “Throw out the servant and her son, for the son of the servant will not inherit with the son of the free woman.” So then, brothers, we are not children of a servant, but of the free woman.
slang_text: But just like back then, the kid born the fleshly way went after the kid born through the Spirit — and it's the exact same deal now. But what's Scripture actually say? "Kick out the slave woman and her son, 'cause the slave woman's son ain't gonna inherit nothin' alongside the free woman's son." So there you go, brothers — we're not kids of some slave woman, we're kids of the free woman.

web_text: Stand firm therefore in the liberty by which Christ has made us free, and don’t be entangled again with a yoke of bondage. Behold, I, Paul, tell you that if you receive circumcision, Christ will profit you nothing. Yes, I testify again to every man who receives circumcision, that he is a debtor to do the whole law. You are alienated from Christ, you who desire to be justified by the law. You have fallen away from grace.
slang_text: So stand your ground in the freedom Christ set us free with, and don't get tangled back up in some bondage yoke. Check it, I'm Paul, and I'm tellin' you straight: if you go get circumcised, Christ won't do you one bit of good. Yeah, I'll say it again to every dude who gets circumcised — he's now on the hook to keep the WHOLE law, all of it. You've cut yourselves off from Christ, you guys tryin' to get made right by the law. You've fallen clean outta grace.

web_text: For we, through the Spirit, by faith wait for the hope of righteousness. For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision amounts to anything, nor uncircumcision, but faith working through love. You were running well! Who interfered with you that you should not obey the truth? This persuasion is not from him who calls you.
slang_text: 'Cause we, through the Spirit, by faith, are waitin' on the hope of righteousness. In Christ Jesus, bein' circumcised don't amount to squat, and bein' uncircumcised don't neither — what matters is faith workin' through love. You guys were runnin' this race so good! Who cut you off so you'd stop obeyin' the truth? Whatever's talked you into this, it ain't comin' from the One who called you.

web_text: A little yeast grows through the whole lump. I have confidence toward you in the Lord that you will think no other way. But he who troubles you will bear his judgment, whoever he is. But I, brothers, if I still preach circumcision, why am I still persecuted? Then the stumbling block of the cross has been removed. I wish that those who disturb you would cut themselves off.
slang_text: A little bit of yeast works its way through the whole batch of dough. I'm confident in the Lord that you're not gonna think any other way. But whoever's stirrin' up trouble for you is gonna carry his own judgment, whoever he is. And me, brothers — if I'm still out here preachin' circumcision, why am I still gettin' persecuted? That'd mean the cross ain't a stumblin' block no more. Honestly, I wish those guys stirrin' you up would just go cut themselves off completely — way harsh, but they earned it.

web_text: For you, brothers, were called for freedom. Only don’t use your freedom for gain to the flesh, but through love be servants to one another. For the whole law is fulfilled in one word, in this: “You shall love your neighbor as yourself.” But if you bite and devour one another, be careful that you don’t consume one another. But I say, walk by the Spirit, and you won’t fulfill the lust of the flesh.
slang_text: 'Cause you, brothers, got called for freedom. Just don't use that freedom as an excuse to feed the flesh — instead, serve each other through love. The whole law boils down to one line: "Love your neighbor like you love yourself." But if you're bitin' and chewin' each other up, watch out, or you'll end up wipin' each other out completely. So here's what I'm sayin': walk by the Spirit, and you won't cave to the flesh's cravings.

web_text: For the flesh lusts against the Spirit, and the Spirit against the flesh; and these are contrary to one another, that you may not do the things that you desire. But if you are led by the Spirit, you are not under the law. Now the deeds of the flesh are obvious, which are: adultery, sexual immorality, uncleanness, lustfulness, idolatry, sorcery, hatred, strife, jealousies, outbursts of anger, rivalries, divisions, heresies,
slang_text: 'Cause the flesh craves stuff against the Spirit, and the Spirit's dead set against the flesh — they're totally at war with each other, so you can't just go do whatever you feel like. But if the Spirit's leadin' you, you're not stuck under the law no more. Now the flesh's deeds are plain to see, no hidin' 'em: cheatin' around, sexual immorality, grody uncleanness, lust, worshippin' idols, dabblin' in witchcraft, hatred, fightin', jealousy, blowin' up in anger, rivalries, splittin' into cliques, chasin' bogus teachings,

web_text: envy, murders, drunkenness, orgies, and things like these; of which I forewarn you, even as I also forewarned you, that those who practice such things will not inherit God’s Kingdom. But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith, gentleness, and self-control. Against such things there is no law. Those who belong to Christ have crucified the flesh with its passions and lusts.
slang_text: envy, murder, gettin' wasted, wild orgies, and stuff just like that. I'm warnin' you now, same as I warned you before: anybody livin' like that ain't gonna inherit God's Kingdom, period. But the fruit the Spirit grows is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faith, gentleness, and self-control. There's no law out there against stuff like that. And the ones who belong to Christ have already crucified the flesh, cravings and all.

web_text: If we live by the Spirit, let’s also walk by the Spirit. Let’s not become conceited, provoking one another, and envying one another.
slang_text: If we're livin' by the Spirit, let's walk by the Spirit too, every step. Let's not get all stuck-up on ourselves, pokin' at each other and gettin' jealous of one another.

web_text: Brothers, even if a man is caught in some fault, you who are spiritual must restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness; looking to yourself so that you also aren’t tempted. Bear one another’s burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christ. For if a man thinks himself to be something when he is nothing, he deceives himself. But let each man examine his own work, and then he will have reason to boast in himself, and not in someone else.
slang_text: Brothers, even if some dude gets caught slippin' up, you spiritual folks gotta bring him back gentle-like, watchin' your own step so you don't get tempted too. Carry each other's baggage, and that way you'll fulfill the law of Christ. 'Cause if some guy thinks he's all that when he's really nothin', he's just conned himself. Everybody oughta check out their own work, and then they'll have somethin' to brag about in themselves, not in comparin' to somebody else.

web_text: For each man will bear his own burden. But let him who is taught in the word share all good things with him who teaches. Don’t be deceived. God is not mocked, for whatever a man sows, that he will also reap. For he who sows to his own flesh will from the flesh reap corruption. But he who sows to the Spirit will from the Spirit reap eternal life.
slang_text: 'Cause everybody's gotta carry their own load. But whoever's gettin' taught the word oughta share the good stuff with whoever's teachin' 'em. Don't be bogus about this — you can't con God. Whatever a guy plants, that's exactly what he's gonna harvest. Plant seeds for the flesh, you're gonna harvest rot straight outta the flesh. Plant seeds for the Spirit, you're gonna harvest eternal life straight outta the Spirit.

web_text: Let us not be weary in doing good, for we will reap in due season, if we don’t give up. So then, as we have opportunity, let’s do what is good toward all men, and especially toward those who are of the household of the faith. See with what large letters I write to you with my own hand. As many as desire to make a good impression in the flesh compel you to be circumcised; just so they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christ.
slang_text: Let's not get burned out doin' good, 'cause we're gonna harvest a great crop when the time's right, long as we don't quit on it. So whenever we get the chance, let's do good to everybody, especially the folks in our own faith family. Check out these big ol' letters I'm writin' with my own hand. Whoever's tryin' to look good on the outside is the one forcin' you to get circumcised — just so they can dodge gettin' persecuted for Christ's cross.

web_text: For even they who receive circumcision don’t keep the law themselves, but they desire to have you circumcised, that they may boast in your flesh. But far be it from me to boast, except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world. For in Christ Jesus neither is circumcision anything, nor uncircumcision, but a new creation. As many as walk by this rule, peace and mercy be on them, and on God’s Israel.
slang_text: 'Cause even the circumcised guys don't keep the law themselves, they just want you circumcised so they can brag about it, usin' your own body as their trophy. But no way am I gonna brag about anything, 'cept the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, through which the whole world's been crucified to me, and me to the world. In Christ Jesus, circumcision don't amount to nothin', and neither does bein' uncircumcised — what matters is a brand-new creation. Whoever's walkin' by this rule, peace and mercy be on 'em, and on God's own Israel.

web_text: From now on, let no one cause me any trouble, for I bear the marks of the Lord Jesus branded on my body. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.
slang_text: From here on out, don't nobody give me no more grief — I'm already carryin' the Lord Jesus' own marks, branded right into my body. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit, brothers. Amen.
